---
title: "prac"
author: "Bo"
date: "2026-04-27"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```



```{r}
traindata = read.table(file = "7406train.csv", sep=",")
```


```{r}
X1 = traindata[,1]
X2 = traindata[,2]

muhat = apply(traindata[,3:202], 1, mean)
Vhat  = apply(traindata[,3:202], 1, var)
```


```{r}
data0 = data.frame(X1 = X1, X2=X2, muhat = muhat, Vhat = Vhat)
head(data0)
dim(data0)
```


```{r}
testdata  = read.table(file = "7406test.csv", sep=",");
test.data = data.frame(testdata)
head(testdata)
View(data0)
View(test.data)

```


```{r}
names(test.data) <- c("X1", "X2")
head(test.data)
dim(test.data)
```


```{r cars}
library(dplyr)
```


```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
# Polynomial Regression
model.mu.poly = lm(muhat ~ poly(X1, 3) + poly(X2, 3) + X1:X2, data = data0)
#model.mu.poly
```


```{r}
pred.mu.poly = predict(model.mu.poly, newdata = test.data)
length(pred.mu.poly)
```


```{r}
#Generalized Additive Model (GAM): Good balance of flexibility + interpretability.

library(mgcv)

model.mu.gam = gam(muhat ~ s(X1) + s(X2) + ti(X1, X2), data = data0)
#model.mu.gam
```


```{r}
pred.mu.gam = predict(model.mu.gam, newdata = test.data)
length(pred.mu.gam)
```



```{r}
# Random Forest (very strong baseline)
library(randomForest)

model.mu.rf = randomForest(muhat ~ X1 + X2, data = data0)
#model.mu.rf
```


```{r}
pred.mu.rf = predict(model.mu.rf, newdata = test.data)
length(pred.mu.rf)
```


```{r}
# Gradient Boosting
library(gbm)

model.mu.gbm = gbm(
  muhat ~ X1 + X2,
  data = data0,
  distribution = "gaussian",
  n.trees = 500,
  interaction.depth = 3
)
```


```{r}
pred.mu.gbm = predict(model.mu.gbm, newdata = test.data)
length(pred.mu.gbm)
```




```{r}
# Varance
# Variance is positive and often skewed, so model log variance
data0$log.var.hat = log(data0$Vhat)
```


```{r}
model.var.gam = gam(log.var.hat ~ s(X1) + s(X2) + ti(X1, X2), data = data0)
```


```{r}
model.var.rf = randomForest(log.var.hat ~ X1 + X2, data = data0)
```


```{r}

```



```{r}
# Predict mean
mu.pred = predict(model.mu.gam, newdata = testdata)
length(mu.pred)
# Predict log variance and convert back
log.var.pred = predict(model.var.gam, newdata = testdata)
var.pred = exp(log.var.pred)
```


```{r}
library(caret)

train_control = trainControl(method = "cv", number = 5)

model.mu.cv = train(
  muhat ~ X1 + X2,
  data = data0,
  method = "rf",
  trControl = train_control
)
```



```{r}

```
